product_overview: A staff-facing admissions platform for the Egyptian Police Academy that lets authorized users sign in, access permitted internal apps, manage applicants and reference data, review analytics, and configure admission setup. The product is organized around role-based access and admin workflows within a single authenticated shell.
core_goals: Sign in as a staff member and reach the correct authorized workspace
Access the authenticated hub and navigate to permitted internal applications
Review admissions analytics and operational reports
Search, filter, and inspect applicant records
Manage lookup dictionaries used by the system
Configure admission setup settings for the active cycle
Move between admin surfaces according to assigned role and access permissions

features.name: Staff authentication and RBAC routing
features.description: Allows staff to sign in with a selected role, enter credentials, and be routed to the appropriate authorized area based on access rights.
user_flows: Navigate to /staff-login -> Select a staff role -> Fill in username -> Fill in password -> Submit login form -> See the authenticated hub or admin surface
Navigate to /staff-login -> Select a staff role that does not permit the requested area -> Submit login form -> See access routed away from the denied area

features.name: Authenticated staff hub
features.description: Provides the main launcher for authorized staff after login, from which permitted internal applications can be reached.
user_flows: Navigate to /hub -> See the authenticated app launcher -> Open a permitted internal application
Navigate to /staff-login -> Sign in successfully -> See the hub as the default authenticated landing area

features.name: Admin reports command center
features.description: Displays high-level admissions analytics, departmental breakdowns, operational status, governance indicators, and alerts for super admins.
user_flows: Navigate to /admin/reports -> Review KPI cards -> Review funnel analytics -> Review department breakdowns -> Review operational status and alerts
Navigate to /staff-login -> Sign in as super admin -> Open /admin/reports -> Review the command center dashboard

features.name: Applicant administration
features.description: Lets authorized users work with applicant records through list review, search, filtering, and record inspection.
user_flows: Navigate to /admin/applicants -> Review the applicant list -> Search applicants -> Filter applicants -> Open an applicant record -> Review status and timeline
Navigate to /admin/applicants -> Review applicant statistics -> Narrow the list using search or filters -> Inspect a matching applicant

features.name: Lookups management
features.description: Provides typed lookup dictionary management with row-level add, edit, activation control, ordering, and guarded deletion behavior.
user_flows: Navigate to /admin/lookups -> Select a lookup type -> Add a lookup row -> Edit a lookup row -> Toggle active state -> Reorder rows
Navigate to /admin/lookups -> Select a lookup type -> Open an existing row for editing -> Save the updated lookup entry
Navigate to /admin/lookups -> Select a lookup type -> Attempt to remove a referenced row -> See deletion blocked by reference constraints

features.name: Admission setup wizard
features.description: Guides authorized staff through the admission setup process for the active cycle, including application settings and related configuration steps.
user_flows: Navigate to /admin/admission-setup/wizard/application_settings -> Select the active cycle context -> Review category settings -> Edit allowed settings -> Save updates -> Continue to the next wizard step
Navigate to /admin/admission-setup/wizard/application_settings -> Review the current application settings summary -> Make permitted changes -> Save the configuration

tech_stack.server_state: TanStack Query 5 with feature query wrappers

tech_stack.client_state: Zustand 4.5 persisted auth store in sessionStorage key pa-auth

tech_stack.styling: Tailwind 3.4 plus RTL Arabic Heritage Modern CSS tokens

tech_stack.components: Radix primitives wrapped in shared components, lucide-react icons, inline SVG charts

routes.description: Staff login page with role selector and username/password form.
Authenticated app launcher for permitted staff apps.
Admin landing route; super admin is sent to reports and other admin roles to dashboard.
Reports command center analytics dashboard for super admins.
Applicant management list, filtering, details, and admin operations.
Lookup dictionary hub and typed lookup row management.
Admission setup wizard application settings step.
Grade import, preview, normalization, adjustments, and overrides.
Committee management surface inside admin shell.

features.name: Staff authentication and RBAC routing
features.description: Role-driven staff login, persisted session, app authorization, and redirects for denied routes.
user_interactions: Select a staff role such as super_admin.
Verify redirect to /hub or the admin surface.

features.name: Admin reports command center
features.description: Enterprise dashboard with KPIs, funnel, department breakdown, operational status, governance, and alerts.
user_interactions: Review KPI cards, charts, filters, and alert sections.

features.name: Applicant administration
features.description: Search, filter, inspect, create/edit, and transition applicant records.

features.name: Lookups management
features.description: Typed lookup dictionary sections, drawer-based add/edit, FK guarded delete, ordering, and status toggles.
user_interactions: Toggle active state or reorder rows.

features.name: Admission setup wizard
features.description: Six-step cycle setup flow for application settings, fees, exams, committees, notifications, and electronic declaration.

known_limitations.issue: Local frontend is currently running through Vite dev server, so TestSprite frontend coverage may be capped by TestSprite's dev-server safety limits.
known_limitations.impact: Fewer generated frontend tests than a production preview run.

known_limitations.issue: Login uses a demo role selector in addition to username/password; automated tests may need explicit instructions to select super_admin before submit.
known_limitations.impact: Generic login automation can fail if it only fills credentials.

known_limitations.issue: The local admin backend was started with EF InMemory and sparse seed data for some normalized admin-record modules.
known_limitations.impact: Frontend pages that expect populated staging-like data may render empty or error states locally.
